metadata.name: usd_deposit_3m
metadata.description: QuantLib-parity USD 3M money-market deposit on a committed discount curve.
metadata.source_detail: QuantLib-Python 1.42.1 parity check using IborIndex forecast fixing for deposit par rate, CashFlows.npv for start and maturity flows, and central finite-difference DV01 on a deterministic USD discount curve. Phase-2 envelope migration (2026-05-09): wrapped in market_envelope with the curve carried in initial_market.curves and an empty plan.steps. The QuantLib oracle's synthetic time pillars are not bootstrap-reproducible; true-calibration migration would require >1e-12 par_rate tolerance. The envelope wrap still routes through engine::execute_with_diagnostics, so the fixture exercises the calibration entry point's plumbing. Regenerated 2026-06-11 (docs/reviews/2026-06-09-core-quant-review.md): core npv now excludes flows on or before the valuation date (market-standard holder-view position value), so the T+0 initial -10MM notional exchange on the 2026-04-30 valuation date is no longer part of npv. New expected npv = prior QuantLib trade NPV (2877.927119438455) + 10,000,000 excluded initial flow at DF=1; equivalently QuantLib CashFlows.npv with includeSettlementDateFlows=False. deposit_par_rate and dv01 are unchanged (the t=0 flow has DF=1 and no rate sensitivity).
metadata.regen_command: manual QuantLib-Python oracle; see inputs.source_reference for reproduction formulas and intermediate values

plan.description: Tier-A wrap: deposit is priced against a pre-built USD-OIS curve carried in initial_market. The curve cannot be bootstrapped from a real-date deposit quote at par_rate tolerance 1e-12 because the snapshot uses synthetic time pillars (0.25, 0.5, ..., 5.0) that don't align with the calibration engine's date arithmetic. Routing through engine::execute_with_diagnostics still validates the envelope schema and the calibration entry point with no calibration-side math change.
